info.title: Gurobi Cluster Manager API
info.description: The Gurobi Cluster Manager API enables to control a Cluster Manager.

post.description: In order to use most of the REST API endpoints, the caller must be authenticated. You can use API keys, and in that case you need to provide the headers 'X-GUROBI-ACCESS-ID' and 'X-GUROBI-SECRET-KEY' at each call. You can also use a JWT, and pass it with each call in the header 'X-GUROBI-JWT'. In order to get a JWT, this endpoint must be called with the user credentials. The JWT is returned in the 'X-GUROBI-JWT' header. The JWT has an expiration time that is generated according to the server configuration parameter 'JWT_EXPIRATION'. If the JWT expires, access will be refused and the error code 401 will be returned. A new JWT can be generated by calling this endpoint again.

get.summary: Get the profile of the authenticated  user

put.summary: Update the profile of the authenticated user

post.summary: Change the password of the authenticated user

get.summary: List API keys of the authenticated user
get.description: An API key enables applications to be authenticated by the Cluster Manager. You can create multiple keys for each account, for example to distinguish between different applications. You can also rotate keys in your applications by creating a new one, and after updating your application, you can delete the old keys. This endpoint lists all the keys onwed by the authenticated user.

post.summary: Generate new key for the authenticated user
post.description: An API key enables applications to be authenticated by the Cluster Manager. You can create multiple keys for each account, for example to distinguish between different applications. You can also rotate keys in your applications by creating a new one, and after updating your application, you can delete the old keys. This endpoint generates an new API access key for current user. It will return the the access key and the associated secret. The secret must be kept client side, it will not be possible to display it again.

delete.summary: Delete an API key of the authenticated user
delete.description: An API key enables applications to be authenticated by the Cluster Manager. You can create multiple keys for an account, for example to distinguish between different applications. You can also rotate keys in your applications by creating a new one, and after updating your application, you can delete the old keys. Once a key has been deleted, application access using this key is permanently revoked and cannot be recovered. This endpoint deletes a key that was created by the current user.

put.summary: Updates an API key of the authenticated user
put.description: An API key enables applications to be authenticated by the Cluster Manager. You can create multiple keys for an account, for example to distinguish between different applications. You can also rotate keys in your applications by creating a new one, and after updating your application, you can delete the old keys. Once a key has been deleted, application access using this key is permanently revoked and cannot be recovered. This endpoint deletes a key that was created by the current user.

get.summary: Get the list of users - SYSADMIN

post.description: The system administrator is in charge of declaring users. Each user has a username, an initial password, a first and last name, an email and a role (READONLY, STANDARD, ADMIN or SYSADMIN). The role defines the access control to some features. Globally speaking, a standard user is only able to submit jobs, batches, and display information about the cluster. An adminitsrator is able to abort any jobs. A system administartor is able to perform all actions such as managing the users, API keys and the cluster. Once created a user ID is returned as part of the 'LOCATION' header. Note that the username cannot be changed.

put.description: The system administrator can update the first name, last name, email and role of any user. The username cannot be changed.

delete.description: The system administrator is able to delete any user.

post.summary: Rename or merges the username for the provided access ID  - SYSADMIN
Activate user API Keys Access - SYSADMIN

204.description: The User API Keys were enabled.

delete.summary: Disable a user API Keys Access  - SYSADMIN

204.description: The User API Keys was disabled.

post.description: The system administrator is able to change the password of any user.

get.description: An API key enables applications to be authenticated by the Cluster Manager. You can create multiple keys for each account, for example to distinguish between different applications. You can also rotate keys in your applications by creating a new one, and after updating your application, you can delete the old keys. This endpoint lists all the keys from all users.

post.description: An API key enables applications to be authenticated by the Cluster Manager. You can create multiple keys for each account, for example to distinguish between different applications. You can also rotate keys in your applications by creating a new one, and after updating your application, you can delete the old keys. This endpoint generates an new API access key for any account. It will return the the access key and the associated secret. The secret must be kept client side, it will not be possible to display it again.

delete.description: An API key enables applications to be authenticated by the Cluster Manager. You can create multiple keys for an account, for example to distinguish between different applications. You can also rotate keys in your applications by creating a new one, and after updating your application, you can delete the old keys. Once a key has been deleted, application access using this key is permanently revoked and cannot be recovered. With this endpoint, the system administartor can delete keys for any accounts.

put.description: An API key enables applications to be authenticated by the Cluster Manager. You can create multiple keys for an account, for example to distinguish between different applications. You can also rotate keys in your applications by creating a new one, and after updating your application, you can delete the old keys. Once a key has been deleted, application access using this key is permanently revoked and cannot be recovered. This endpoint deletes a key that was created by the current user.

post.summary: Activate an API Key Access - SYSADMIN

delete.summary: Disable an API Key Access - SYSADMIN

get.description: Returns the list of nodes of the cluster with the latest status and metrics.
This endpoint is provided to test if a given node is reachable

post.summary: Start job processing on a node - SYSADMIN

delete.summary: Stop job processing on a node - SYSADMIN

get.description: Returns exported configuration properties. This endpoint will not return all the configuration properties, but only a subset in order to monitor the configuration.

post.description: Updates dynamic configuration properties, such as the job limit

get.description: This endpoint provides the stored node metrics, if no dates range or filter is provided it returns the last 30 days

Returns the license information from each node of the cluster. This call is useful to verify and monitor licenses across the cluster nodes. For example, the expiration date of licenses can easily be monitored.

get.summary: Lists the jobs from the history
get.description: Returns the list of job descriptions from the history. This endpoint provides several query parameters to filter and control the returned list. As the list could be large, we recommend defining a page length of 200 jobs. You can specify the starting job index, with the start parameter. You can also specify a different page length with the length parameter. For example, if you set the page length to 10, you will need to call the endpoint several times with the start indices of 0, 10, 20... until the returned list is empty. If the start index and the page length are not specified, all the jobs jobs will be returned. The jobs are sorted by end date (endedAt), and you can specify if you want an ascending or descending ordering using the sort parameter. For convenience, the filter parameter provides you with quick filters: last 24 hours (24h), last 7 days (7d), last 30 days (30d). Otherwise you can specify a date range with from and to parameters. Dates can be specified using a number (number of milliseconds since 1/1/1970 GMT) or a string using the ISO8601 format. In addition, you can filter by user ID, username, job ID, batch ID or job status

parameters.name: batchId
parameters.description: Filter by Batch ID (* for any batch, - for no batch)

parameters.name: app
parameters.description: Filter by application name (Use keyword 'undefined' to retrieve jobs with no app defined)

get.description: This endpoint returns the state of a specific jobs from the history.

This endpoint returns the list of all jobs started, queued or recently processed in the cluster. You can filter the jobs by using the query parameters queued, running or completed. In the returned list, the same job can appear multiple times as it may have been queued in multiple nodes of the cluster unless the aggregate query parameter is true .
This endpoint returns the state of a specific jobs. It returns a list because the job may have been queued in multiple nodes of the cluster, unless the aggregate query parameter is true

200.description: A job singleton or multiple entries

delete.summary: Aborts a job - ADMIN or owner
delete.description: This endpoint kills a running job immediately or remove it from the queue from all cluster members.

204.description: The job has been aborted or did not exist

post.summary: Interrupts a job - ADMIN or owner
post.description: This endpoint interrupts a running job from all cluster members.

204.description: The job has been interrupted, did not exist or the runtime is not supported

get.summary: Returns the log of an active job
get.description: Returns the engine log of the specified running or completed job. This endpoint is valid only with the node where the job is being processed. Several query parameters can be used to get the log from the job start, last lines or from a given index. The response header X-GUROBI-INDEX returns the last known index to be passed in the next call, so that new lines can be retrieved incrementally.

404.description: The machine or job was not found.

get.summary: Returns the metrics of an active job
get.description: Returns the metrics of the specified running or completed job. This endpoint is valid only with the node where the job is being processed. The returned data is formatted as a text file with one json object per line. Each object gives the timestamp since the start of the optimizatuon and one or more attibutes for the metrics such as objective value and bound. Several query parameters can be used to get the metrics from the job start, last lines or from a given index. The response header X-GUROBI-INDEX returns the last known index to be passed in the next call, so that new lines can be retrieved incrementally.

get.summary: Returns the parameters of an active job
get.description: Returns the parameters from a running or completed job. This endpoint is valid only with the node where the job is being processed.

The Cluster Manager can store objects (model files, parameter files) that can be used in batches. Each object has a name and belongs to a container, similar to a file name and a directory in a file system. Shared objects can be created and used in many batches, and they must be deleted explicitly. Non-shared objects are owned by one or more batches and will be deleted when the no more used. This endpoint returns the list of object descriptors. This endpoint provides several query parameters to filter and control the returned list. As the list could be large, the default page length is 200 objects. You can specify the starting object index, with the start parameter. You can also specify a different page length with the length parameter. For example, if you set the page length to 10, you will need to call the endpoint several times with the start indices of 0, 10, 20... until the returned list is empty. If the start index and the page length are not specified, the last 200 objects will be returned. The objects are sorted by creation date (createdAt), and you can specify if you want an ascending or descending ordering using the sort parameter. For convenience, the filter parameter provides you with quick filters: last 24 hours (24h), last 7 days (7d), last 30 days (30d). Otherwise you can specify a date range with from and to parameters. Dates can be specified using a number (number of milliseconds since 1/1/1970 GMT) or a string using the ISO8601 format. In addition, you can filter by user ID, username, object ID, container name, and object name.

post.description: Declares a new object. Once declared, the content must be uploaded, and then closed.

delete.summary: Deletes an object - ADMIN or owner

parameters.name: force
parameters.description: Force delete of objects that may be in use

put.description: The object must exists and this endpoint will upload the content in one part. If multiple parts are necessary, use the multipart upload endpoint instead.

post.description: Indicates that all the content has been uploaded. This lets the client upload multiple parts or retry even a single part. Once the client application considers that it has uploaded all the content, it must use this endpoint to close the object. Once an object is closed, content cannot be uploaded or changed anymore.

put.summary: Uploads a part for multipart objects

get.description: A batch is an optimization request that can be submitted, monitored and retrieved later on. Compared to regular optimization requests, a batch does need to maintain a connection between the client and the server. The batch is submitted with all the input data and parameters, then the batch can be monitored until completion. Once completed, the results can be downloaded. This endpoint lists the batches stored in the server. This endpoint provides several query parameters to filter and control the returned list. As the list could be large, the default page length is 200 bacthes. You can specify the starting batch index, with the start parameter. You can also specify a different page length with the length parameter. For example, if you set the page length to 10, you will need to call the endpoint several times with the start indices of 0, 10, 20... until the returned list is empty. If the start index and the page length are not specified, the last 200 jobs will be returned. The batches are sorted by creation date (createdAt), and you can specify if you want an ascending or descending ordering using the sort parameter. For convenience, the filter parameter provides you with quick filters: last 24 hours (24h), last 7 days (7d), last 30 days (30d). Otherwise you can specify a date range with from and to parameters. Dates can be specified using a number (number of milliseconds since 1/1/1970 GMT) or a string using the ISO8601 format. In addition, you can filter by user ID, username, batch ID or batch status

parameters.name: discarded
parameters.description: Filter by discarded status (true, false, all)

post.description: A batch is an optimization request that can be submitted, monitored and retrieved later on. Compared to regular optimization requests, a batch does need to maintain a connection between the client and the server. The batch is submitted with all the input data and parameters, then the batch can be monitored until completion. Once completed, the results can be downloaded. This endpoint creates a new batch. Once created, the input data can be uploaded and attached using other endpoints, before being submitted.

get.description: A batch is an optimization request that can be submitted, monitored and retrieved later on. Compared to regular optimization requests, a batch does need to maintain a connection between the client and the server. The batch is submitted with all the input data and parameters, then the batch can be monitored until completion. Once completed, the results can be downloaded. This endpoint returns a batch descriptor.

delete.summary: Discards batch data - ADMIN or owner
delete.description: A batch is an optimization request that can be submitted, monitored and retrieved later on. This endpoint discards references to input and output data. Once discarded, the batch cannot be submitted and data cannot be downloaded, however the batch remains in the system until it is automatically purged from the history.

post.description: A batch is an optimization request that can be submitted, monitored and retrieved later on. This endpoint submits the batch for processing. Before calling this endpoint, all input data objects must be uploaded and attached to the batch.

delete.summary: Aborts a batch - ADMIN or owner
delete.description: A batch is an optimization request that can be submitted, monitored and retrieved later on. This endpoint aborts the batch for processing.

post.summary: Adds an object to a batch - owner
post.description: A batch is an optimization request that can be submitted, monitored and retrieved later on. This endpoint attaches an input object that was uploaded. if the object was not closed, it is automatically closed and owned by this batch.

put.summary: Save the settings, if more than one instance of the Cluster Manager is running - SYSADMIN

get.summary: Get the password policy settings, SYSADMIN also gets Authentication Provider settings with concealed credentials and System Settings

post.summary: Test authentication provider configurations without storing any settings on the db - SYSADMIN

parameters.name: providerTest
parameters.description: The provider and login object to test

get.summary: Generates a Service Provider Metadata file or a configuration json object based on the accept header, the settings must have been saved previously and the selected Authentication provider should be SAML - SYSADMIN

204.description: Not enough saved settings to generate file

200.description: Generated XML or JSON metadata information

post.summary: Parse an Identity Provider Metadata file an returns the required information - SYSADMIN

parameters.name: metadata
parameters.description: The metadata xml file provided by the Identity Provider

wlsJobConfigurations.description: WLS On-Demand list of allowed Job configurations

wlsInterrupt.description: WlS On-Demand maximum allowed seconds before interrupting jobs if the proxy connection is lost

wlsJobConfig.description: Default WLS On-Demand configuration elected by the token

nodeThreadLimit.description: Thread Limit (maximum number of running threads)
New node thread limit, use 0 to disable

NodeType.description: Node type (1=COMPUTE: compute server, 2=WORKER: distributed worker)

jobLimit.description: Job Limit (maximum number of jobs running)

nodeThreadLimit.description: Thread Limit (maximum number of running threads)

nbUsedThreads.description: Total number of reserved threads by all running jobs, if enabled

nbRecentJobs.description: Number of recent jobs in the short term history

processedJobCounter.description: Persistent counter of processed jobs [0,4294967295], will cycle

idleTime.description: Idle time since the last job execution (in minutes)

memory.description: Percentage of memory currently used on the machine

cpu.description: Percentage of CPU currently used on the machine

JobProperties.description: Job properties defined when submitting the job

parentId.description: Parent job ID for distributed jobs

runtime.description: Requested runtime to execute the job

strict.description: Indicates if runtime version must match strictly the requested version

keepAliveTimeout.description: Indicates a custom value for the keep alive timeout (seconds)

idleTimeout.description: Indicates a custom value for the idle timeout (seconds)

apitype.description: API used by the client (gurobi_cl, grbcluser, Python, Java...)

threadLimit.description: If supported, max number of threads for the requested job

solcnt.description: Current count of feasible solutions found

cutcnt.description: Current count of cutting planes applied

StoredNodeMetric.description: Metric Object by node and type

unit.description: The unit (in milliseconds) the metrics are being stored

buckets.description: All the metrics for each hour  if recorded

NodeMetricsBucket.description: A bucket contains an hour of metrics

timestampHour.description: The timestamp with the start of the hour recorded in UTC

x.description: All the minutes recorded on that hour

queue.description: Jobs in the Node queue metric

running.description: Jobs running ath the same time metric

MetricData.description: Recorded data for a metric in a bucket

values.description: All the values recorded on that hour by the minute in te same position as x array

port.description: LDAP server port as string. If empty it will default to the standard ports (389 for ldap or 636 for ldaps)

bindDN.description: Represents LDAP DN for searching for the user DN. Typically read only user DN.

bindPassword.description: LDAP password for searching for the user DN.

baseDN.description: LDAP domain to use for users.

filter.description: Filter for the User Object Filter. Must use %s for the username. To scape special character like the parenthesis use "\(" or its unicode value \28

usernameAttribute.description: Used for mapping the unique username identifier. If no unique username attribute is specified, it will default to using uid. It's important that the attribute name matches the one used in the filter template.

firstNameAttribute.description: Used for mapping the first name

lastNameAttribute.description: Used for mapping the last name

secure.description: Indicates if LDAPS will be used

tlsInsecure.description: Indicates if LDAPS will validate certificate signatures

tlsCerts.description: All the Certification Authority certificates. You may need to export all the certs in the CA (such as root CA and intermediate CA) in the certification path, just add them one after another

idpURL.description: Identity Provider Single Sign-On service URL, The endpoint where the CLuster Manager sends the SAML Authentication request

idpid.description: Entity ID or Issuer URL of the Identity Provider (IdP)

requestBinding.description: The SAML Authentication Request Protocol binding used to send the authentication request

spid.description: Unique identifier that will identify Cluster Manager

spURL.description: The Cluster Manager URL, please input the full access URL for the cluster Manager.

firstNameAttr.description: SAML assertion for the First Name

lastNameAttr.description: SAML assertion for the Last Name

usernameAttr.description: SAML assertion for the username, it should be unique

certs.description: List of Certificates provided by the IdP

idpURL.description: Identity Provider Single Sign-On service URL, The endpoint where the CLuster Manager sends the SAML Authentication request

idpid.description: Entity ID or Issuer URL of the Identity Provider (IdP)

requestBinding.description: The SAML Authentication Request Protocol binding used to send the authentication request

certs.description: List of Certificates provided by the IdP

PasswordPolicy.description: Provides all the settings to define Password Strength

symbols.description: Enforce of Special Symbols like !, @, #, $, etc. characters

disableCaseSensitivity.description: Used to specify the case sensitivity validation on unique username identifier for any authentication provider.

logoutPage.description: Used to specify the url to be redirected after the logout action is performed. It will redirect to the login page by default.

provider.description: Authentication provider selection 1 GRB_PROVIDER, 2 LDAP_PROVIDER, 3 SAML_PROVIDER

minAge.description: Min Age (in minutes) of last user synchronization to be updated, only when the authentication provider supports User Sync

batch.description: How many users will be synced per batch, only when the authentication provider supports User Sync

frequency.description: How frequent (in minutes) the users will be synced, only when the authentication provider supports User Sync

jwtExpiration.description: The duration of the JWT Token in minutes

historyMaxAge.description: Limit the job history to a number of days.

metricsMaxAge.description: Limit the metrics history to a number of days.

metricsRefresh.description: The poll interval (seconds) when the metrics will be retrieved, they will be stored by the max number within the minute.

notClosedMaxAge.description: Limit the time an object must be closed before being deleted (hours)

bulkDeleteSize.description: Set the number of elements to be removed by the cleaning process, set by default at 20

ProviderTestResult.description: The result of testing the authentication provider

valid.description: Describes if the Authentication test is valid

message.description: The information about the test messages

isError.description: Indicates if the message is an error

messages.description: Contains all the Test result messages, it can include success and error messages

ProviderTest.description: Tests authentication provider configurations without storing any settings on the db

updatedAt.description: Last time when the settings were updated

sysAdminLogin.description: Indicates if the user is a SYSADMIN

LoginAuthConfig.description: Public information about the selected authentication provider

disableCaseSensitivity.description: Indicates if the username case sensitivity is disabled

loginPage.description: Indicates the generated SAML Login page if SAML Authentication provider is configured

oldpassword.description: Old user's password to be replaced

UserPasswd.description: Data to change password of loggin user

disabled.description: Indicates if the User has been disabled

disabledReason.description: Indicates why the User has been disabled - SYSADMIN

apiKeysDisabled.description: Indicates if the User's API Keys has been disabled

skipTour.description: Indicates if the User's disabled the tour permanently

merge.description: If the target username already exists, force a merge

skipTour.description: Indicates if the User's disabled the tour permanently

ApiKeyUpdate.description: Elements required to update an API Key

NewApiKey.description: API key with secret only returned after creation

name.description: Name of the object, must be unique in a container

discarded.description: Indicates if batch data was discarded

runtime.description: Requested runtime to execute the batch

apitype.description: API used by the client (gurobi_cl, grbcluser, Python, Java...)

resultFiles.description: List of files requested for output

jobs.description: List of jobs executed for this batch

threadLimit.description: Threads reservation for the the submission

wlsConfig.description: WlS On-Demand Compute Server Job configuration

storeId.description: Unique object id attached to the batch
Unique object id attached to the batch

runtime.description: Requested runtime to execute the batch

apitype.description: API type of the client submitting the job (C++, .Net, Python...)

threadLimit.description: Threads reservation for the the submission

wlsConfig.description: WlS On-Demand Compute Server Job configuration

resultFiles.description: List of files requested for output
